title: May the Fourth Be With You Day
story: Every May 4, Star Wars fans around the world celebrate Star Wars Day with the famous phrase "May the Fourth be with you!" This clever play on words comes from the movie quote "May the Force be with you." Fans dress up as their favorite characters like Luke Skywalker, Princess Leia, or Darth Vader. Movie theaters show Star Wars marathons, and people share memes and videos online. Some fans build lightsaber battles in their backyards or bake Death Star cakes. Even Disney parks throw special Star Wars parties with parades and fireworks to celebrate this galactic holiday!
activity_question: What famous movie quote inspired the Star Wars Day phrase?
activity_choices: May the Force be with you
I am your father
Help me, Obi-Wan Kenobi
Do or do not, there is no try
activity_answer: May the Force be with you
bonus_fact: Disney officially recognizes Star Wars Day and celebrates it at their theme parks worldwide.

title: Heroes in Red: International Firefighters' Day
story: Sirens wail and red trucks race through the streets as communities worldwide honor their bravest heroes on May 4 during International Firefighters' Day! People visit fire stations to thank firefighters, bring them homemade cookies, and watch exciting demonstrations of ladder trucks and rescue equipment. Kids get to try on heavy helmets, sit in fire trucks, and learn about fire safety. Many firefighters wear red ribbons or special patches to remember fallen heroes who risked everything to save others. Social media fills with photos of local fire departments and heartfelt thank-you messages from grateful families.
activity_question: What do people often bring to firefighters on International Firefighters' Day?
activity_choices: Homemade cookies
Birthday cakes
Pizza slices
Ice cream sundaes
follow_up_question: What would you want to ask a firefighter if you visited their station?

title: Squeeze the Day with Orange Juice!
story: Every May 4, people across America celebrate National Orange Juice Day by raising their glasses to this sunny citrus drink! Families start their morning with fresh-squeezed OJ, while restaurants offer special orange juice cocktails and smoothies. Some people try making their own juice from scratch, discovering just how many oranges it takes to fill one glass. Social media fills with photos of colorful orange drinks and fun facts about vitamin C. It's the perfect day to appreciate this breakfast favorite!
activity_question: What do people often discover when making orange juice from scratch?
activity_choices: How many oranges it takes to fill one glass
That oranges are actually blue inside
That orange juice tastes better at night
How to grow orange trees indoors
activity_answer: How many oranges it takes to fill one glass
bonus_fact: It takes about 3-4 medium oranges to make just one 8-ounce glass of orange juice!

title: Sweet and Citrusy Celebration Day
story: Candied orange peels might sound weird, but they're actually a sweet treat that people celebrate every May 4! These chewy, sugary strips are made by cooking orange peels in syrup until they become candy-like. Bakers love adding them to cookies, cakes, and chocolates for a zesty twist. Some people make their own at home, while others hunt for them at fancy candy shops or bakeries. It's a perfect way to use up orange peels instead of throwing them away!
activity_question: How are candied orange peels made?
activity_choices: By cooking orange peels in syrup
By freezing orange peels overnight
By painting orange peels with food coloring
By mixing orange peels with ice cream
activity_answer: By cooking orange peels in syrup
bonus_fact: Candied orange peels can stay fresh for up to six months when stored properly!

title: Weather Watchers Get Their Day!
story: Every May 4, weather observers across the country get a special shout-out for their amazing work! These dedicated people spend their days tracking temperatures, measuring rainfall, and watching storm clouds roll in. They help meteorologists predict if you'll need an umbrella tomorrow or if it's perfect beach weather. Many weather stations hold open houses where kids can see cool instruments like wind gauges and rain collectors. It's the perfect day to thank the folks who help keep us safe from storms!
activity_question: What do weather observers help meteorologists predict?
activity_choices: Tomorrow's weather
Earthquake locations
Ocean tides
Plant growth rates
follow_up_question: What weather instrument would you most want to try using?

title: Cluck Yeah! Chicken Respect Day
story: Chickens get their moment to shine every May 4 on National Respect for Chickens Day! People celebrate these feathered friends by learning amazing chicken facts, sharing cute photos online, and visiting farm sanctuaries. Some families even throw chicken-themed parties with egg-shaped treats. This special day reminds everyone that chickens are smart, social animals who deserve kindness and care, not just dinner plates!
activity_question: How do people celebrate National Respect for Chickens Day?
activity_choices: By learning chicken facts and visiting farm sanctuaries
By having a chicken dance contest
By painting their houses yellow
By wearing feather hats to school
activity_answer: By learning chicken facts and visiting farm sanctuaries
bonus_fact: Chickens can recognize over 100 different faces and have great memories!
